nodes.label: Learning About Island Threats
nodes.description: Characters discover information about dangers threatening Stormwreck Isle while at the cloister

nodes.label: Player Character Introduction
nodes.description: DM encourages players to introduce their characters and discuss reasons for visiting Dragon's Rest

nodes.label: Marching Order Discussion
nodes.description: DM requests players to establish their marching order as they prepare to approach the cloister

nodes.label: Environmental Inquiry Resolution
nodes.description: Players consult map 2 and "Dragon's Rest Locations" to understand the cloister's visible features from the boat

nodes.label: Exploration Toward Cloister
nodes.description: Party begins movement toward the cloister while organizing their formation

nodes.label: Approach to Dragon's Rest
nodes.description: Adventurers sail toward the island, noting vibrant seaweed and illuminated landscapes

nodes.label: Anchor Drop at Harbor
nodes.description: Ship anchors at the harbor entrance while observing cliffs and rocky formations

nodes.label: Shore Leave by Sailors
nodes.description: Sailors transport adventurers to the dock where they bid farewell

nodes.label: Statue Inspection
nodes.description: Adventurers examine the central statue featuring a wizened man and songbirds

nodes.label: Path Discovery
nodes.description: Identification of the stone-paved trail ascending the cliffside to the temple

nodes.label: Directional Guidance
nodes.description: Sailors point toward the path and offer wishes for safe travels

nodes.label: Initial_Zombie_Encounter
nodes.description: The party faces three shambling zombies originating from a shipwreck, presented with the choice to fight or continue along the upward path.

nodes.label: Combat_Initiated
nodes.description: Upon deciding to engage, the zombies begin moving toward the party, initiating a combat sequence with initiative checks and melee attacks.

nodes.label: Strategic_Movement_Decision
nodes.description: The party chooses to bypass the zombies by advancing along the upward path, avoiding direct conflict.

nodes.label: Hearing Ruckus
nodes.description: Characters hear splashing and a wet, gurgling moan behind them

nodes.label: Encounter with Drowned Sailors
nodes.description: Three figures shambling up from the water's edge wearing sailor attire, with gray skin and seawater dripping from their mouths

nodes.label: Observing Drowned Figures
nodes.description: Characters notice the figures' unnatural appearance, including gray skin, wet clothing, and predatory lurching motion toward them

nodes.label: Zombie Resists Death
nodes.description: A zombie's Undead Fortitude trait causes it to survive lethal damage, prompting the DM to inform players about the phenomenon

nodes.label: Radiant Damage Effect
nodes.description: A zombie reacts painfully to radiant damage (like sacred flame), allowing the DM to explain its vulnerability to radiant energy

nodes.label: Intelligence Check for Zombie Tactics
nodes.description: Players must make Intelligence checks to learn that critical hits or radiant damage bypasses Undead Fortitude

nodes.label: Combat_Between_Zombies_And_Adventurers
nodes.description: The zombies engage in combat with the adventurers

nodes.label: Adventurers_Wake_Up_In_Temple
nodes.description: The characters awaken in the temple after being rescued

nodes.label: Runara_Rescues_Adventurers
nodes.description: Runara arrives to rescue the adventurers from the zombies

nodes.label: Dialogue_With_Runara
nodes.description: Runara explains she heard combat sounds and arrived to prevent the zombies from dragging them into the sea

nodes.label: Observing_Combat_Sounds
nodes.description: Runara notices the sounds of combat leading her to intervene

nodes.label: Encountering_Zombies
nodes.description: Characters encounter zombies and choose not to fight, allowing easy escape.

nodes.label: Observing_Zombies_Not_Following
nodes.description: Characters observe that zombies do not follow them up the path toward Dragon's Rest.

nodes.label: Curious_Kobold_Questions
nodes.description: The kobolds repeatedly ask questions, prompting the characters to insist they stop.

nodes.label: Encounter_with_Elder_Runara
nodes.description: After silencing the kobolds, Elder Runara approaches to greet the characters.

nodes.label: Dialogue_about_Hospitality
nodes.description: Elder Runara acknowledges the characters’ service (if zombies were defeated) or invites them to stay at Dragon’s Rest, specifying accommodations in areas A1/A5 and shared meals in A3.

nodes.label: Offer_of_Gifts_Accepted
nodes.description: If characters propose paying or serving in exchange for lodging, Elder Runara accepts their offerings.

nodes.label: Access_to_Basic_Rules_Equipment
nodes.description: The characters learn they can purchase equipment from Myla, referencing resources in the Basic Rules.

nodes.label: Arriving_at_Cloister
nodes.description: The adventurer's arrival attracts the attention of the cloister's inhabitants, consisting primarily of kobolds and a couple of distant observers.

nodes.label: Dialogue_with_Kobolds
nodes.description: A curious kobold initiates conversation by asking the adventurer's name, prompting rapid questioning from the rest of the kobolds.

nodes.label: Kobolds Fall Silent
nodes.description: The chattering kobolds cease their noise as an elderly human woman descends from the cloister.

nodes.label: Welcome Dialogue
nodes.description: The elderly human woman extends her arms in greeting and addresses the adventurers with a welcome message referencing Bahamut's guidance.

nodes.label: Initial Encounters With Runara
nodes.description: Runara meets the characters with indifference, as stated in basic rules social interaction

nodes.label: Zombie Combat On Beach
nodes.description: Characters engage in battle against zombies threatening the cloister

nodes.label: Friendly Transition From Indifference
nodes.description: Runara becomes friendly after characters prove concern for cloister's safety through actions

nodes.label: Hostile Reaction To Resident Harm
nodes.description: Runara turns hostile if characters injure cloister residents, demanding reparations

nodes.label: Observation Of Hidden Residence
nodes.description: Residences note Runara's constant presence in public spaces while maintaining secrecy about her aquatic habitat

nodes.label: Inquiry About Living Arrangements
nodes.description: Characters ask about Runara's dwelling, prompting humorous denials from cloister inhabitants

nodes.label: Kitchen Incident Involving Laylee
nodes.description: An incident in the kitchen resulted in Laylee being prohibited from handling Myla's alchemical fire.

nodes.label: Initial_Dialogue_with_Tarak
nodes.description: Characters interact with Tarak, who is initially friendly but becomes hostile if questioned about his past

nodes.label: Exploration_of_Sea_Caves
nodes.description: Tarak ventures to the sea caves to harvest heart cap mushrooms from myconids

nodes.label: Observation_of_Fungal_Octopus_Guardian
nodes.description: Tarak notices a fearsome fungus-covered octopus monster guarding the myconid caves

nodes.label: Recognition_of_Gilded_Gallows_Symbol
nodes.description: A successful Intelligence (History) check identifies the gold hangman's noose tattoo as a symbol of the Gilded Gallows

nodes.label: Past_Poisoner_Discovered
nodes.description: Characters learn of Tarak's former role as a ruthless poisoner for the Gilded Gallows

nodes.label: Observing Ship Crash
nodes.description: Varnoth witnesses a ship altering its course and crashing into northern rocks during her temple maintenance duties

nodes.label: Historical Information Discovery
nodes.description: Learning Varnoth's name and succeeding on a History check reveals information about her past as General Varnoth Wender and the Azure Wolves

nodes.label: Attitude Shift Through Dialogue
nodes.description: Engaging Varnoth in conversations about history, ethics, and global impacts shifts her disposition from indifferent to friendly

nodes.label: Temple Maintenance Activity
nodes.description: Varnoth regularly performs repairs and upkeep tasks at the temple using her mason's tools

nodes.label: Observation of Garden Plots
nodes.description: Small, well-maintained garden areas containing flowers, herbs, and vegetables are noticed along the path.

nodes.label: Discovery of Plaza Structure
nodes.description: A widened plaza located approximately thirty feet above the bay is identified, featuring architectural elements like doorways.

nodes.label: Observation of Statue
nodes.description: A serene stone statue depicting a dragon is noted midway along the plaza.

nodes.label: Identification of Doorways
nodes.description: Six open doorways carved into the cliff face are detected during examination.

nodes.label: Observes Bronze Dragon Depiction
nodes.description: A character successfully identifies the dragon statue as depicting a bronze dragon after examining it

nodes.label: Dialog About Statue Identity
nodes.description: Characters question Runara about the statue; she reveals it portrays Astalagan, who died on the cliffs centuries ago, but keeps secret that Astalagan was her father

nodes.label: Exploring_Cloisters_Cells
nodes.description: Characters investigate the monastic cells within the cloister, examining their layouts and contents.

nodes.label: Discovered_Vacant_Cell
nodes.description: Characters learn the westernmost cell is unoccupied and potentially usable.

nodes.label: Observed_Mylas_Cluttered_Cell
nodes.description: Characters notice Myla's cell is disorganized, containing scattered junk and tools.

nodes.label: Noticed_Hammock_Equipped_Cells
nodes.description: Characters observe the fifth and sixth cells feature hammocks designated for the kobold inhabitants.

nodes.label: Observing Favorite Cooking Days
nodes.description: The cloister residents express quiet fondness for the days when Tarak cooks, although they never verbalize it

nodes.label: Dialogue_Between_Tarak_And_Varnoth
nodes.description: Tarak and Varnoth read and discuss various works in the library.

nodes.label: Observation_Of_Myla_Studious_Nature
nodes.description: Myla is described as studious.

nodes.label: Discovering_the_Library
nodes.description: Adventurers discover a spacious library behind an oak door with iron bands

nodes.label: Exploring_the_Room
nodes.description: Adventurers explore the library, noting bookshelves lining every wall

nodes.label: Observing_Specific_Features
nodes.description: Adventurers notice three free-standing shelves in the western half, a central table with two benches, writing implements, book stands, and glass-shielded lamps in the eastern half

nodes.label: Recognizing Bahamut
nodes.description: Character successfully identifies Bahamut after examining the statue with a Religion check

nodes.label: Detect Magic Revelation
nodes.description: Casting detect magic reveals a faint aura around the statue

nodes.label: Magical Protection Activation
nodes.description: Non-evil creatures gain bonus dice on saving throws due to protective magic

nodes.label: Offering Maintenance
nodes.description: Runara and assistants tend to the temple's incense offerings and maintain its upkeep

nodes.label: Historical Awareness
nodes.description: Runara recognizes the lingering magical effect from her late father Astalagan's death

nodes.label: Observing_Serenity
nodes.description: A feeling of serenity pervades the temple

nodes.label: Initial_Conversation_with_Residents
nodes.description: Characters engage in discussions with Dragon's Rest residents about the cloister's challenges

nodes.label: Exploration_of_Dragons_Rest
nodes.description: Party investigates Dragon's Rest area

nodes.label: Introduction_to_Seagrow_Caves_Adventure
nodes.description: Residences reveal potential adventures in the sea caves

nodes.label: Introduction_to_Compass_Rose_Wreck
nodes.description: Discussions hint at the shipwreck adventure

nodes.label: Journey_to_Clifftop_Observatory
nodes.description: Final destination after completing cave and wreck expeditions

nodes.label: Confrontation_with_Sparkrender
nodes.description: Climactic showdown against Sparkrender in the observatory

nodes.label: Character_Goal_Interaction
nodes.description: Personal objectives influence interactions with Runara and other inhabitants

nodes.label: Hearing Cries For Help
nodes.description: Adventurers hear cries for help while staying at the cloister

nodes.label: Second Zombie Encounter
nodes.description: Adventurers confront three zombies threatening Blepp and Tarak

nodes.label: Dialogue With Runara
nodes.description: Characters discuss zombies with Runara who reveals the shipwreck connection

nodes.label: Discovering Shipwreck Source
nodes.description: Runara informs characters that a wrecked ship off the rocks is the zombie origin

nodes.label: Fleeing Up Lower Path
nodes.description: Two residents flee upward along the lower path

nodes.label: Stained Robes Observed
nodes.description: Blood and dirt stains their clothing from the struggle

nodes.label: Abandoned Fishing Gear Found
nodes.description: Their fishing equipment is discovered discarded behind them

nodes.label: Pursuit By Sailor Corpses
nodes.description: Three bloated corpse-sailors begin chasing the fleeing residents

nodes.label: Using Zombie Encounter
nodes.description: The DM recommends using zombie encounters to introduce additional combat challenges during the adventure

nodes.label: Dialogue About Quest
nodes.description: Tarak instructs the characters to investigate the sea caves, uncover the plight of the myconids, retrieve heart cap mushrooms, and prepare for potential conflict with the fungal octopus.

nodes.label: Receiving Gifted Sacks
nodes.description: Tarak presents the adventurers with a foul-smelling sack of food scraps as a token of goodwill toward the myconids.

nodes.label: Receiving Potions
nodes.description: Tarak provides the adventurers with two vials of healing potion for their journey.

nodes.label: Journey To Sea Caves
nodes.description: The party travels to the sea caves to fulfill Tarak’s request and assess the situation with the myconids.

nodes.label: Observing Guardian Threat
nodes.description: The adventurers note the presence of the fungal octopus, a dangerous guardian constructed by the myconids.

nodes.label: Combat Against Fungal Octopus
nodes.description: The party engages in battle against the fungal octopus to secure entry into the myconid territory.

nodes.label: Witnessing Recent Wreck
nodes.description: Varnoth and Rix observe a ship abruptly veer off course and crash into the rocks

nodes.label: Suggesting Investigation
nodes.description: Varnoth and Rix advise the characters to investigate the cause of the crash to help the island

nodes.label: Consulting Runara
nodes.description: When questioned by the characters, Runara recommends examining the wreck of Compass Rose

nodes.label: Arriving_at_Temple
nodes.description: Characters arrive at the temple (area A5) summoned by Runara

nodes.label: Dialog_with_Runara
nodes.description: Runara reveals the history of dragon-linked sites and discusses Aidron's disappearance

nodes.label: Receiving_Moonstone_Key
nodes.description: Runara hands over the moonstone key to unlock the observatory

nodes.label: Learning_of_Aidrons_Argument
nodes.description: Adventurers learn Aidron rejected Runara's teachings and fled to the observatory

nodes.label: Proving_Trustworthiness
nodes.description: Characters demonstrate competence by overcoming zombies, myconids, and navigating the shipwreck

nodes.label: Dialogue With Runara
nodes.description: Runara explains her true nature as a bronze dragon, references the island's unresolved conflicts, and requests assistance

nodes.label: Sudden Transformation Observed
nodes.description: A flash of lightning causes Runara to transform from a human woman into an enormous bronze dragon, revealing her true form

nodes.label: Unsuccessful_Encounter_On_Island
nodes.description: An encounter on the island results in all characters becoming unconscious.

nodes.label: Awakening_In_Temple
nodes.description: The characters awaken in the temple, discovering they are tended to by kobolds.

nodes.label: Sidekick_Introduction
nodes.description: Additional characters join the party as sidekicks upon arriving at Dragon's Rest.

nodes.label: Exploration of Stormwreck Isle
nodes.description: Characters traverse the island, uncovering uncharted areas and risks beyond designated locations

nodes.label: Runara Refuses Information
nodes.description: Runara declines to give the moonstone key or information about the observatory until the characters complete other objectives on the island.

nodes.label: Conditional Combat Difficulty
nodes.description: The text notes that encountering the observatory prematurely results in overly challenging combat encounters, suggesting a potential future conflict.

nodes.label: Observatory Destroyed By Battle
nodes.description: Runara battled her blue dragon rival, reducing the observatory to rubble.

nodes.label: Aidron Arrives At Observatory
nodes.description: Aidron traveled from Dragon's Rest to investigate the observatory.

nodes.label: Conflict Between Aidron And Sparkrender
nodes.description: Aidron and Sparkrender engaged in a fierce fight upon their first meeting.

nodes.label: Sparkrender Attempts To Influence Aidron
nodes.description: Sparkrender tried to manipulate Aidron against Runara, but failed due to Aidron's prejudice.

nodes.label: Sparkrender Captures Aidron
nodes.description: Sparkrender subdued and imprisoned Aidron in the observatory ruins.

nodes.label: Exploration_Via_Rowboat
nodes.description: Adventurers take a rowboat from Dragon's Rest to the Clifftop Observatory, sailing around the eastern end of the island.

nodes.label: Exploration_Along_Coast
nodes.description: Adventurers walk around the coastline of the island, circumventing the eastern bay to reach the Clifftop Observatory.

nodes.label: Exploration_Overland
nodes.description: Adventurers traverse a rough path across the island's rocky terrain from Dragon's Rest to the head of the eastern bay, directly approaching the Clifftop Observatory.

nodes.label: Observation_of_Sparkrender
nodes.description: Characters observe Sparkrender flying by, causing unease due to potential detection

nodes.label: Lightning_Breath_Revelation
nodes.description: Character identifies crystalline protrusions/scarred vegetation as signs of lightning strikes or dragon breath via Nature check

nodes.label: Approach_to_Observatory
nodes.description: Characters move toward the observatory after Sparkrender's departure

nodes.label: Exploring Rocky Terrain
nodes.description: Adventurers climb over rocky ground on Stormwreck Isle

nodes.label: Discovering Crystalline Protrusions
nodes.description: Adventurers spot glassy crystal protrusions emerging from the earth

nodes.label: Observing Reddish Scarred Vegetation
nodes.description: Adventurers notice vegetation with reddish branching scars forming similar shapes to the crystals

nodes.label: Hearing Screech and Sighting Winged Creature
nodes.description: A screeching roar prompts adventurers to see a winged, glittering blue creature soaring overhead

nodes.label: Sparkrender Does Not Notice Characters
nodes.description: Sparkrender fails to notice the characters as he retreats to his lair

nodes.label: Climb Bluffs to Reach D1
nodes.description: Characters move along the bluffs toward area D1

nodes.label: Ascend Pillar to Access D2-D5
nodes.description: By securing the boat at a pillar, characters gain access to areas D2, D3, D4, or D5

nodes.label: Exploring Southeast Tip
nodes.description: Adventurers round the southeastern tip of the island and observe crumbling ruins atop basalt columns off the main island body.

nodes.label: Observing Winged Creature
nodes.description: Adventurers hear a screeching roar and notice a winged, glittering blue shape soaring through the air.

nodes.label: Encountering Celestial Being
nodes.description: The winged, glittering blue creature descends onto the column farthest from Stormwreck Isle and vanishes from view.

nodes.label: kobold_hostility_observation
nodes.description: Observers notice kobolds display hostility toward outsiders but often issue warnings instead of immediate attacks

nodes.label: charisama_check_dialogue_attempt
nodes.description: Characters attempt to negotiate with kobolds using Charisma checks (Deception/Intimidation/Persuasion skills)

nodes.label: dragon_argument_encounter
nodes.description: Sparkrender argues with the metallic-green dragon at the observatory tower

nodes.label: secret_plan_discovery
nodes.description: Characters learn of Sparkrender's plan to manifest full power tied to the sculpture in rotunda ruins

nodes.label: dragon_activity_observation
nodes.description: Kobolds report hearing violent activity from the metallic dragon in the observatory tower

nodes.label: Magical_Light_Sparks
nodes.description: Magical light sparks ignite in both statues' open mouths when the key is inserted

nodes.label: Bridge_Formation_Discovered
nodes.description: A shimmering bridge of iridescent magical energy emerges connecting the overlook to the observatory ruins

nodes.label: Exploring Path To Cliff Edge
nodes.description: Adventurers traverse a broken and overgrown path toward the edge of the cliff

nodes.label: Observing Marble Statues At Overlook
nodes.description: Adventurers notice two marble statues veined with gold, depicting dragons roaring silently

nodes.label: Initial_Kobold_Stirge_Battle
nodes.description: The two winged kobolds initiate a battle with eight stirges.

nodes.label: Stirges_Shift_Attention_To_Characters
nodes.description: Six of the stirges notice the characters and redirect their attacks toward them.

nodes.label: Kobolds_Handle_Remaining_Stirges
nodes.description: The kobolds eliminate the last two stirges before assessing the characters’ actions.

nodes.label: Character_Action_Affects_Kobold_Response
nodes.description: The kobolds react to whether characters aid, attack, or avoid them, altering their stance accordingly.

nodes.label: Kobolds_Approach_Characters_Post_Victory
nodes.description: Surviving kobolds approach the characters, revealing themselves as Mek and Minn.

nodes.label: Revelation_of_Mek_and_Minns_Backstory
nodes.description: Mek and Minn explain their abandonment of their wounded sister, Myla, and their allegiance to Sparkrender.

nodes.label: Conditional_Friendly_Dialogue_with_Kobolds
nodes.description: If characters aided the kobolds or hinted at Myla’s survival, the kobolds become allies and offer guidance.

nodes.label: Retrieval_of_Moonstone_Key_from_D1
nodes.description: Characters obtain the moonstone key from Area D1 to enable progress to D4.

nodes.label: Bridge_Activation_to_D4
nodes.description: Using the moonstone key, characters unlock access to the bridge connecting to Area D4.

nodes.label: Observing Plaza Structure
nodes.description: The plaza features broken stone lines, fragments of statues, pillars, and a central sculpture.

nodes.label: Hearing Screech
nodes.description: A loud screech erupts from across the plaza.

nodes.label: Encountering Batlike Creatures and Kobolds
nodes.description: A half-dozen batlike creatures swarm around two winged kobolds with blue-painted snouts.

nodes.label: Kobolds vs. Batlike Creatures Combat
nodes.description: The kobolds engage in fierce combat against the batlike creatures but struggle to prevail.

nodes.label: Observing_Golden_Sculpture
nodes.description: A character studies the sculpture and succeeds in an Intelligence (Arcana) check to decipher its significance.

nodes.label: Discovering_Comet_Trail
nodes.description: The adventurers learn that the comet's position predicts it will pass extremely close to Toril.

nodes.label: Planning_Ritual_For_Kingkiller_Star
nodes.description: Sparkrender intends to perform a ritual when the comet (King-Killer Star) reaches its closest point to Toril, believing it grants control over dragons.

nodes.label: Stirge_Attack_Interrupts_Task
nodes.description: A stirge attack disrupts the kobolds' preparations for Sparkrender's ritual.

nodes.label: Kobolds_Explain_Dragon_Effigies
nodes.description: If asked, Mek and Minn reveal the kobolds created the dragon effigies based on Sparkrender's instructions.

nodes.label: Encountering_Kobold_Group
nodes.description: The kobolds are hostile toward the characters and determined to scare off intruders.

nodes.label: Intimidation_Successful
nodes.description: A successful Charisma (Intimidation) check causes the kobolds to back down.

nodes.label: Discovering_Sling_Bullet
nodes.description: The characters observe the kobolds polishing the Sling Bullet.

nodes.label: Exploring_Bridge_Path
nodes.description: Characters traverse the rickety bridge linking the rotunda to the kobold camp.

nodes.label: Observing_Ritual_Preparations
nodes.description: Characters notice the kobolds actively preparing for Sparkrender's ritual.

nodes.label: Observing_Skittering_Noises
nodes.description: Adversaries detect skittering sounds and whispered voices emanating from within the ruined tower

nodes.label: Exploring_Ruins_Structure
nodes.description: Adversaries examine the deteriorated tower and note gaps sealed with wooden planks and frayed fabric

nodes.label: Observation_Sparkrender_Sleeping
nodes.description: Adventurers notice Sparkrender the blue dragon wyrmling sleeping against the northeast wall

nodes.label: Discovery_Caved_In_Wall_to_D6
nodes.description: Players discover a caved-in wall beneath the tower revealing access to the secret library (area D6)

nodes.label: Pulley_Lift_Construction_By_Kobolds
nodes.description: A rough pulley lift created by kobolds allows wingless members to ascend to the main floor

nodes.label: Observation_Light_Effects_On_Walls
nodes.description: Rays of light dance across the stained glass dome, creating iridescent color shifts on the crumbling walls

nodes.label: Observation_Star_Map_Floor
nodes.description: A gilded star map covers the dust-covered floor, formed by intricate lines and inlays

nodes.label: Observation_Scholar_Statues
nodes.description: Four tall alabaster statues depicting scholars face different cardinal directions

nodes.label: Observation_Blue_Dragon_Slumbering
nodes.description: A lithe blue dragon sleeps in the northeast corner, surrounded by scattered treasure

nodes.label: Observation_Lightning_Around_Dragon
nodes.description: Arcs of electricity flicker along the dragon's horns and snout

nodes.label: Exploring to Avoid Awakening Dragon
nodes.description: Characters move quietly around the area to avoid waking the Blue Dragon Wyrmling, requiring successful stealth checks.

nodes.label: Attempting to Turn Statues
nodes.description: Characters attempt to turn north and east statues without waking Sparkrender. Failed checks result in noisy activation that awakens the dragon.

nodes.label: Awakening_Sparkrender
nodes.description: Sparkrender awakens and becomes hostile toward the characters, growling and threatening them in Draconic.

nodes.label: Skill_Check_Attempt
nodes.description: Characters who know Draconic attempt to persuade Sparkrender via Deception, Intimidation, or Persuasion skills. A success halts the attack temporarily.

nodes.label: Failed_Intervention_Or_Mention_Of_Names
nodes.description: If no skill check succeeds or if Aidron/Runara is mentioned, Sparkrender attacks.

nodes.label: Breath_Weapon_Attack
nodes.description: Sparkrender uses its Breath Weapon on subsequent rounds, requiring cover from statues.

nodes.label: Dragon_Dialogue_Turns
nodes.description: Sparkrender delivers thematic dialogue each turn, expressing ambition and disdain for the characters.

nodes.label: Low_HP_Flight_Response
nodes.description: When reduced to ≤10 HP, Sparkrender vows vengeance and attempts to flee, disengaging mid-combat.

nodes.label: Character_Flee_Response
nodes.description: Sparkrender taunts retreating characters, mocking their inability to resist his power.

nodes.label: Post_Defeat_Noise_Discovery
nodes.description: After defeating Sparkrender, characters hear growling/yelling emanating from Area D6.

nodes.label: Discover_Clue_In_Isolated_Study
nodes.description: Characters find a clue exhorting "four scholars" to "point your eyes toward the Dragon's light"

nodes.label: Identify_Dragon_Constellation_On_Starmap
nodes.description: Character successfully identifies a dragon-shaped constellation in the southeast quadrant of the star map after investigating

nodes.label: Notice_Grooves_On_Statues
nodes.description: Character detects worn grooves indicating statues can pivot on their bases after performing a perception check

nodes.label: Detect_Alignment_With_Seasonal_Qualifier
nodes.description: Successful perception check while adjusting statues reveals slight settling when aligned with southeast quadrant

nodes.label: Activate_Hidden_Mechanism
nodes.description: All statues correctly oriented trigger a glowing section to descend forming a spiral staircase to the library

nodes.label: Seal_Library_Shut
nodes.description: Staircase ascends when any statue is displaced, lifting anyone on it back to the observatory level

nodes.label: GatheringTreasureAfterDefeat
nodes.description: Characters gather Sparkrender's hoard after he no longer defends it

nodes.label: sparkrender_traps_aidron
nodes.description: Sparkrender weakens Aidron, forces him into the library, and collapses a wall to seal the exit, trapping him

nodes.label: characters_clear_rubble
nodes.description: Characters attempt to remove rubble blocking the library entrance by climbing from area D5 or swimming to the base

nodes.label: discovery_of_secret_library_interior
nodes.description: Characters successfully clear rubble to reveal the library's interior

nodes.label: meeting_aidron
nodes.description: Characters greet Aidron, who explains his failed escape attempts and conflict with Sparkrender

nodes.label: aidrons_dialogue_with_characters
nodes.description: Aidron reveals his regrets about failing to defeat Sparkrender and fears of becoming a conduit for Sparkrender's powers

nodes.label: aidron_decides_to_confront_sparkrender
nodes.description: Aidron prepares to challenge Sparkrender directly despite knowing his weakness

nodes.label: Environmental_Observation
nodes.description: Adversaries detect stale air with the scent of old parchment, observe book-filled shelves, and note shattered glass cases scattering contents across the stone floor

nodes.label: Encounter_Bronze_Dragon
nodes.description: An agitated bronze dragon emerges from wreckage near an overturned desk, indicating recent impact

nodes.label: Exploring_Treasure_Room
nodes.description: Characters explore the library room, searching for concealed items

nodes.label: Discovering_Magical_Items
nodes.description: Successful Investigation check reveals the presence of a +1 battleaxe and a 2nd-level hold person spell scroll

nodes.label: Detecting_Item_Location
nodes.description: Casters use detect magic spell to locate hidden items without requiring an ability check

nodes.label: Receiving_Catalog_Info
nodes.description: Aidron provides directional guidance to the discovered magical items

nodes.label: Return_to_Observatory_During_Ritual
nodes.description: Characters arrive at the observatory to find Sparkrender's ritual underway

nodes.label: Drive_Sparkrender_Away_Encounter
nodes.description: Characters force Sparkrender off the island without killing him

nodes.label: Leave_Aidron_Imprisoned
nodes.description: Characters choose to leave Aidron imprisoned in area D6

nodes.label: Runara_Urges_Return
nodes.description: Runara instructs characters to return to the observatory

nodes.label: Discover_Replacement_Effigies
nodes.description: Characters find crude replacements for dragon effigies if previously destroyed

nodes.label: Confront_Sparkrender
nodes.description: Final showdown with Sparkrender, who fights to the death

nodes.label: Sparkrender_Unleash_BreathWeapon
nodes.description: Sparkrender releases lightning-based Breath Weapon attack as characters approach

nodes.label: Notice_Kobold_Presence
nodes.description: Characters detect lingering kobolds and winged kobolds near the site

nodes.label: Observing Colorful Light Display
nodes.description: Streams of colored light swirl around the golden statue, matching the colors of the dragon effigies

nodes.label: Lightning Bolt Emission
nodes.description: The blue dragon releases a bolt of lightning upward after displaying signs of pain/ecstasy

nodes.label: Agony of Bound Dragon
nodes.description: The bronze dragon is visibly struggling against three heavy chains tied to the ground

nodes.label: Random Magical Effect During Combat
nodes.description: At the end of each combat round (initiative count 0), a random magical effect occurs due to dragon spirits swirling in the rotunda.

nodes.label: Astalagan's Blessing
nodes.description: Aidron and the characters regain 1d4 + 4 hp as bronze light surrounds them.

nodes.label: Clyssavar's Flames
nodes.description: Sparkrender takes 7 (2d6) fire damage if failing a Dexterity save from golden light crashing into him.

nodes.label: Eldenemir's Gift
nodes.description: Sparkrender's breath weapon recharges as blue light enfolds him.

nodes.label: Sharruth's Fury
nodes.description: Characters take 3 (1d6) fire damage if failing a Dexterity save from red light erupting with fire.

nodes.label: Turadaer's Tricks
nodes.description: Aidron and the characters gain advantage on attack/saving throws until next round’s initiative 0 from brass light sparking around them.

nodes.label: Attempting to Remove Chains
nodes.description: Characters attempt to undo the clasps attaching Aidron's chains to the ground, requiring an action per clasp.

nodes.label: Aidron Regains Mobility
nodes.description: After all three clasps are removed, Aidron is no longer restrained, though his speed is reduced by 10 feet due to lingering chains.

nodes.label: Potential Use of Repulsion Breath
nodes.description: Aidron retains the capability to use his Repulsion Breath if strategically advantageous, despite being unable to target Sparkrender with his lightning Breath Weapon.

nodes.label: Manipulate_Effigies
nodes.description: Characters interact with effigies to invoke effects from the Dragon Spirits Table

nodes.label: Destroy_Effigies
nodes.description: Characters destroy effigies to permanently negate their effects

nodes.label: Disturb_Sculpture
nodes.description: Attempts to alter the golden sculpture cause temporary distractions to Sparkrender

nodes.label: Return to Dragon's Rest
nodes.description: The characters return to Dragon's Rest with Aidron, marking the end of their mission.

nodes.label: Meeting Runara
nodes.description: Runara greets the characters, expressing pleasure at Aidron’s return and offering rewards.

nodes.label: Reward Distribution
nodes.description: Runara grants each adventurer a potion of healing and an exquisite pearl valued at 100 gp.

nodes.label: Conditional Reaction to Sparkrender's Fate
nodes.description: Runara expresses grief if Sparkrender is dead, acknowledging the loss while refraining from blaming the characters.

nodes.label: Future Adventure Options Mentioned
nodes.description: Text hints at potential post-adventure scenarios involving Sparkrender's relatives or discoveries in the secret library.

edges.description: Characters discover information about dangers threatening Stormwreck Isle while at the cloister
DM encourages players to introduce their characters and discuss reasons for visiting Dragon's Rest
DM requests players to establish their marching order as they prepare to approach the cloister
Players consult map 2 and "Dragon's Rest Locations" to understand the cloister's visible features from the boat
Party begins movement toward the cloister while organizing their formation
Adventurers sail toward the island, noting vibrant seaweed and illuminated landscapes
Ship anchors at the harbor entrance while observing cliffs and rocky formations
Sailors transport adventurers to the dock where they bid farewell
Adventurers examine the central statue featuring a wizened man and songbirds
Identification of the stone-paved trail ascending the cliffside to the temple
Sailors point toward the path and offer wishes for safe travels
The party faces three shambling zombies originating from a shipwreck, presented with the choice to fight or continue along the upward path.
Upon deciding to engage, the zombies begin moving toward the party, initiating a combat sequence with initiative checks and melee attacks.
The party chooses to bypass the zombies by advancing along the upward path, avoiding direct conflict.
Characters hear splashing and a wet, gurgling moan behind them
Three figures shambling up from the water's edge wearing sailor attire, with gray skin and seawater dripping from their mouths
Characters notice the figures' unnatural appearance, including gray skin, wet clothing, and predatory lurching motion toward them
The zombies engage in combat with the adventurers
The characters awaken in the temple after being rescued
Runara arrives to rescue the adventurers from the zombies
Runara explains she heard combat sounds and arrived to prevent the zombies from dragging them into the sea
Runara notices the sounds of combat leading her to intervene
Characters encounter zombies and choose not to fight, allowing easy escape.
Characters observe that zombies do not follow them up the path toward Dragon's Rest.
The kobolds repeatedly ask questions, prompting the characters to insist they stop.
After silencing the kobolds, Elder Runara approaches to greet the characters.
Elder Runara acknowledges the characters’ service (if zombies were defeated) or invites them to stay at Dragon’s Rest, specifying accommodations in areas A1/A5 and shared meals in A3.
If characters propose paying or serving in exchange for lodging, Elder Runara accepts their offerings.
The characters learn they can purchase equipment from Myla, referencing resources in the Basic Rules.
The adventurer's arrival attracts the attention of the cloister's inhabitants, consisting primarily of kobolds and a couple of distant observers.
A curious kobold initiates conversation by asking the adventurer's name, prompting rapid questioning from the rest of the kobolds.
The chattering kobolds cease their noise as an elderly human woman descends from the cloister.
The elderly human woman extends her arms in greeting and addresses the adventurers with a welcome message referencing Bahamut's guidance.
Runara meets the characters with indifference, as stated in basic rules social interaction
Characters engage in battle against zombies threatening the cloister
Runara becomes friendly after characters prove concern for cloister's safety through actions
Runara turns hostile if characters injure cloister residents, demanding reparations
Residences note Runara's constant presence in public spaces while maintaining secrecy about her aquatic habitat
Characters ask about Runara's dwelling, prompting humorous denials from cloister inhabitants
An incident in the kitchen resulted in Laylee being prohibited from handling Myla's alchemical fire.
Characters interact with Tarak, who is initially friendly but becomes hostile if questioned about his past
Tarak ventures to the sea caves to harvest heart cap mushrooms from myconids
Tarak notices a fearsome fungus-covered octopus monster guarding the myconid caves
A successful Intelligence (History) check identifies the gold hangman's noose tattoo as a symbol of the Gilded Gallows
Characters learn of Tarak's former role as a ruthless poisoner for the Gilded Gallows
Varnoth witnesses a ship altering its course and crashing into northern rocks during her temple maintenance duties
Learning Varnoth's name and succeeding on a History check reveals information about her past as General Varnoth Wender and the Azure Wolves
Engaging Varnoth in conversations about history, ethics, and global impacts shifts her disposition from indifferent to friendly
Varnoth regularly performs repairs and upkeep tasks at the temple using her mason's tools
Small, well-maintained garden areas containing flowers, herbs, and vegetables are noticed along the path.
A widened plaza located approximately thirty feet above the bay is identified, featuring architectural elements like doorways.
A serene stone statue depicting a dragon is noted midway along the plaza.
Six open doorways carved into the cliff face are detected during examination.
A character successfully identifies the dragon statue as depicting a bronze dragon after examining it
Characters question Runara about the statue; she reveals it portrays Astalagan, who died on the cliffs centuries ago, but keeps secret that Astalagan was her father
Characters investigate the monastic cells within the cloister, examining their layouts and contents.
Characters learn the westernmost cell is unoccupied and potentially usable.
Characters notice Myla's cell is disorganized, containing scattered junk and tools.
Characters observe the fifth and sixth cells feature hammocks designated for the kobold inhabitants.
The cloister residents express quiet fondness for the days when Tarak cooks, although they never verbalize it
Tarak and Varnoth read and discuss various works in the library.
Adventurers discover a spacious library behind an oak door with iron bands
Adventurers explore the library, noting bookshelves lining every wall
Adventurers notice three free-standing shelves in the western half, a central table with two benches, writing implements, book stands, and glass-shielded lamps in the eastern half
Character successfully identifies Bahamut after examining the statue with a Religion check
Casting detect magic reveals a faint aura around the statue
Non-evil creatures gain bonus dice on saving throws due to protective magic
Runara and assistants tend to the temple's incense offerings and maintain its upkeep
Runara recognizes the lingering magical effect from her late father Astalagan's death
A feeling of serenity pervades the temple
Characters engage in discussions with Dragon's Rest residents about the cloister's challenges
Residences reveal potential adventures in the sea caves
Discussions hint at the shipwreck adventure
Final destination after completing cave and wreck expeditions
Climactic showdown against Sparkrender in the observatory
Personal objectives influence interactions with Runara and other inhabitants
Adventurers hear cries for help while staying at the cloister
Adventurers confront three zombies threatening Blepp and Tarak
Characters discuss zombies with Runara who reveals the shipwreck connection
Runara informs characters that a wrecked ship off the rocks is the zombie origin
Two residents flee upward along the lower path
Blood and dirt stains their clothing from the struggle
Their fishing equipment is discovered discarded behind them
Three bloated corpse-sailors begin chasing the fleeing residents
The DM recommends using zombie encounters to introduce additional combat challenges during the adventure
Tarak instructs the characters to investigate the sea caves, uncover the plight of the myconids, retrieve heart cap mushrooms, and prepare for potential conflict with the fungal octopus.
Tarak presents the adventurers with a foul-smelling sack of food scraps as a token of goodwill toward the myconids.
Tarak provides the adventurers with two vials of healing potion for their journey.
The party travels to the sea caves to fulfill Tarak’s request and assess the situation with the myconids.
The adventurers note the presence of the fungal octopus, a dangerous guardian constructed by the myconids.
The party engages in battle against the fungal octopus to secure entry into the myconid territory.
Varnoth and Rix observe a ship abruptly veer off course and crash into the rocks
Varnoth and Rix advise the characters to investigate the cause of the crash to help the island
When questioned by the characters, Runara recommends examining the wreck of Compass Rose
Characters arrive at the temple (area A5) summoned by Runara
Runara reveals the history of dragon-linked sites and discusses Aidron's disappearance
Runara hands over the moonstone key to unlock the observatory
Adventurers learn Aidron rejected Runara's teachings and fled to the observatory
Characters demonstrate competence by overcoming zombies, myconids, and navigating the shipwreck
Runara explains her true nature as a bronze dragon, references the island's unresolved conflicts, and requests assistance
A flash of lightning causes Runara to transform from a human woman into an enormous bronze dragon, revealing her true form
An encounter on the island results in all characters becoming unconscious.
The characters awaken in the temple, discovering they are tended to by kobolds.
Additional characters join the party as sidekicks upon arriving at Dragon's Rest.
Characters traverse the island, uncovering uncharted areas and risks beyond designated locations
Runara declines to give the moonstone key or information about the observatory until the characters complete other objectives on the island.
The text notes that encountering the observatory prematurely results in overly challenging combat encounters, suggesting a potential future conflict.
Runara battled her blue dragon rival, reducing the observatory to rubble.
Aidron traveled from Dragon's Rest to investigate the observatory.
Aidron and Sparkrender engaged in a fierce fight upon their first meeting.
Sparkrender tried to manipulate Aidron against Runara, but failed due to Aidron's prejudice.
Sparkrender subdued and imprisoned Aidron in the observatory ruins.
Adventurers take a rowboat from Dragon's Rest to the Clifftop Observatory, sailing around the eastern end of the island.
Adventurers walk around the coastline of the island, circumventing the eastern bay to reach the Clifftop Observatory.
Adventurers traverse a rough path across the island's rocky terrain from Dragon's Rest to the head of the eastern bay, directly approaching the Clifftop Observatory.
Characters observe Sparkrender flying by, causing unease due to potential detection
Character identifies crystalline protrusions/scarred vegetation as signs of lightning strikes or dragon breath via Nature check
Characters move toward the observatory after Sparkrender's departure
Adventurers climb over rocky ground on Stormwreck Isle
Adventurers spot glassy crystal protrusions emerging from the earth
Adventurers notice vegetation with reddish branching scars forming similar shapes to the crystals
A screeching roar prompts adventurers to see a winged, glittering blue creature soaring overhead
Sparkrender fails to notice the characters as he retreats to his lair
Characters move along the bluffs toward area D1
By securing the boat at a pillar, characters gain access to areas D2, D3, D4, or D5
Adventurers round the southeastern tip of the island and observe crumbling ruins atop basalt columns off the main island body.
Adventurers hear a screeching roar and notice a winged, glittering blue shape soaring through the air.
The winged, glittering blue creature descends onto the column farthest from Stormwreck Isle and vanishes from view.
Observers notice kobolds display hostility toward outsiders but often issue warnings instead of immediate attacks
Characters attempt to negotiate with kobolds using Charisma checks (Deception/Intimidation/Persuasion skills)
Sparkrender argues with the metallic-green dragon at the observatory tower
Characters learn of Sparkrender's plan to manifest full power tied to the sculpture in rotunda ruins
Kobolds report hearing violent activity from the metallic dragon in the observatory tower
Magical light sparks ignite in both statues' open mouths when the key is inserted
A shimmering bridge of iridescent magical energy emerges connecting the overlook to the observatory ruins
Adventurers traverse a broken and overgrown path toward the edge of the cliff
Adventurers notice two marble statues veined with gold, depicting dragons roaring silently
The two winged kobolds initiate a battle with eight stirges.
Six of the stirges notice the characters and redirect their attacks toward them.
The kobolds eliminate the last two stirges before assessing the characters’ actions.
The kobolds react to whether characters aid, attack, or avoid them, altering their stance accordingly.
Surviving kobolds approach the characters, revealing themselves as Mek and Minn.
Mek and Minn explain their abandonment of their wounded sister, Myla, and their allegiance to Sparkrender.
If characters aided the kobolds or hinted at Myla’s survival, the kobolds become allies and offer guidance.
Characters obtain the moonstone key from Area D1 to enable progress to D4.
Using the moonstone key, characters unlock access to the bridge connecting to Area D4.
The plaza features broken stone lines, fragments of statues, pillars, and a central sculpture.
A loud screech erupts from across the plaza.
A half-dozen batlike creatures swarm around two winged kobolds with blue-painted snouts.
The kobolds engage in fierce combat against the batlike creatures but struggle to prevail.
A character studies the sculpture and succeeds in an Intelligence (Arcana) check to decipher its significance.
The adventurers learn that the comet's position predicts it will pass extremely close to Toril.
Sparkrender intends to perform a ritual when the comet (King-Killer Star) reaches its closest point to Toril, believing it grants control over dragons.
The kobolds are hostile toward the characters and determined to scare off intruders.
A successful Charisma (Intimidation) check causes the kobolds to back down.
The characters observe the kobolds polishing the Sling Bullet.
Characters traverse the rickety bridge linking the rotunda to the kobold camp.
Characters notice the kobolds actively preparing for Sparkrender's ritual.
Adversaries detect skittering sounds and whispered voices emanating from within the ruined tower
Adversaries examine the deteriorated tower and note gaps sealed with wooden planks and frayed fabric
Adventurers notice Sparkrender the blue dragon wyrmling sleeping against the northeast wall
Players discover a caved-in wall beneath the tower revealing access to the secret library (area D6)
A rough pulley lift created by kobolds allows wingless members to ascend to the main floor
Rays of light dance across the stained glass dome, creating iridescent color shifts on the crumbling walls
A gilded star map covers the dust-covered floor, formed by intricate lines and inlays
Four tall alabaster statues depicting scholars face different cardinal directions
A lithe blue dragon sleeps in the northeast corner, surrounded by scattered treasure
Arcs of electricity flicker along the dragon's horns and snout
Characters move quietly around the area to avoid waking the Blue Dragon Wyrmling, requiring successful stealth checks.
Characters attempt to turn north and east statues without waking Sparkrender. Failed checks result in noisy activation that awakens the dragon.
Sparkrender awakens and becomes hostile toward the characters, growling and threatening them in Draconic.
Characters who know Draconic attempt to persuade Sparkrender via Deception, Intimidation, or Persuasion skills. A success halts the attack temporarily.
If no skill check succeeds or if Aidron/Runara is mentioned, Sparkrender attacks.
Sparkrender uses its Breath Weapon on subsequent rounds, requiring cover from statues.
Sparkrender delivers thematic dialogue each turn, expressing ambition and disdain for the characters.
When reduced to ≤10 HP, Sparkrender vows vengeance and attempts to flee, disengaging mid-combat.
Sparkrender taunts retreating characters, mocking their inability to resist his power.
After defeating Sparkrender, characters hear growling/yelling emanating from Area D6.
Characters find a clue exhorting "four scholars" to "point your eyes toward the Dragon's light"
Character successfully identifies a dragon-shaped constellation in the southeast quadrant of the star map after investigating
Character detects worn grooves indicating statues can pivot on their bases after performing a perception check
Successful perception check while adjusting statues reveals slight settling when aligned with southeast quadrant
All statues correctly oriented trigger a glowing section to descend forming a spiral staircase to the library
Staircase ascends when any statue is displaced, lifting anyone on it back to the observatory level
Sparkrender weakens Aidron, forces him into the library, and collapses a wall to seal the exit, trapping him
Characters attempt to remove rubble blocking the library entrance by climbing from area D5 or swimming to the base
Characters successfully clear rubble to reveal the library's interior
Characters greet Aidron, who explains his failed escape attempts and conflict with Sparkrender
Aidron reveals his regrets about failing to defeat Sparkrender and fears of becoming a conduit for Sparkrender's powers
Aidron prepares to challenge Sparkrender directly despite knowing his weakness
Adversaries detect stale air with the scent of old parchment, observe book-filled shelves, and note shattered glass cases scattering contents across the stone floor
An agitated bronze dragon emerges from wreckage near an overturned desk, indicating recent impact
Characters explore the library room, searching for concealed items
Successful Investigation check reveals the presence of a +1 battleaxe and a 2nd-level hold person spell scroll
Casters use detect magic spell to locate hidden items without requiring an ability check
Aidron provides directional guidance to the discovered magical items
Characters arrive at the observatory to find Sparkrender's ritual underway
Characters choose to leave Aidron imprisoned in area D6
Runara instructs characters to return to the observatory
Characters find crude replacements for dragon effigies if previously destroyed
Final showdown with Sparkrender, who fights to the death
Sparkrender releases lightning-based Breath Weapon attack as characters approach
Characters detect lingering kobolds and winged kobolds near the site
Streams of colored light swirl around the golden statue, matching the colors of the dragon effigies
The blue dragon releases a bolt of lightning upward after displaying signs of pain/ecstasy
The bronze dragon is visibly struggling against three heavy chains tied to the ground
At the end of each combat round (initiative count 0), a random magical effect occurs due to dragon spirits swirling in the rotunda.
Aidron and the characters regain 1d4 + 4 hp as bronze light surrounds them.
Sparkrender takes 7 (2d6) fire damage if failing a Dexterity save from golden light crashing into him.
Sparkrender's breath weapon recharges as blue light enfolds him.
Characters take 3 (1d6) fire damage if failing a Dexterity save from red light erupting with fire.
Aidron and the characters gain advantage on attack/saving throws until next round’s initiative 0 from brass light sparking around them.
Characters attempt to undo the clasps attaching Aidron's chains to the ground, requiring an action per clasp.
After all three clasps are removed, Aidron is no longer restrained, though his speed is reduced by 10 feet due to lingering chains.
Aidron retains the capability to use his Repulsion Breath if strategically advantageous, despite being unable to target Sparkrender with his lightning Breath Weapon.
Characters interact with effigies to invoke effects from the Dragon Spirits Table
Characters destroy effigies to permanently negate their effects
Attempts to alter the golden sculpture cause temporary distractions to Sparkrender
The characters return to Dragon's Rest with Aidron, marking the end of their mission.
Runara greets the characters, expressing pleasure at Aidron’s return and offering rewards.
Runara grants each adventurer a potion of healing and an exquisite pearl valued at 100 gp.
Runara expresses grief if Sparkrender is dead, acknowledging the loss while refraining from blaming the characters.
Text hints at potential post-adventure scenarios involving Sparkrender's relatives or discoveries in the secret library.
